section: AP 13: Dermatopathology Topics for Anatomic Pathology Residents
allocation_strategy: Clinical judgment prioritizing pattern recognition, major tumors, and high-yield inflammatory conditions

questions.education_objective: Recognize the interface dermatitis reaction pattern and understand its differential diagnosis

questions.topic: Lichen planus vs. lichenoid drug eruption
questions.education_objective: Distinguish lichen planus from drug-induced lichenoid reactions using morphologic criteria

questions.topic: Erythema multiforme vs. toxic epidermal necrolysis
questions.education_objective: Differentiate erythema multiforme from Stevens-Johnson syndrome/TEN using histologic features
Identify the psoriasiform reaction pattern and its characteristic features
Distinguish psoriasis from lichen simplex chronicus using architectural and cellular features
Recognize the spongiotic reaction pattern and understand eczematous processes
Differentiate pemphigus vulgaris from bullous pemphigoid based on blister location and morphology
Distinguish true acantholysis from acantholytic dyskeratosis and understand their significance

questions.topic: Granulomatous reaction patterns: sarcoidal vs. necrobiotic
questions.education_objective: Classify granulomatous reaction patterns and correlate with specific diseases
Differentiate granuloma annulare from necrobiosis lipoidica using histologic features
Recognize vasculopathic changes and classify vascular pathology in skin
Identify leukocytoclastic vasculitis and understand its clinical associations

questions.topic: Basal cell carcinoma: nodular vs. infiltrative subtypes
questions.education_objective: Classify basal cell carcinoma subtypes and understand their prognostic implications

questions.topic: Superficial vs. micronodular basal cell carcinoma
questions.education_objective: Distinguish superficial from micronodular BCC and correlate with treatment planning

questions.topic: Squamous cell carcinoma in situ vs. invasive SCC
questions.education_objective: Distinguish in situ from invasive squamous cell carcinoma and assess invasion depth
Differentiate keratoacanthoma from conventional squamous cell carcinoma

questions.topic: Actinic keratosis vs. squamous cell carcinoma in situ
questions.education_objective: Distinguish actinic keratosis from squamous cell carcinoma in situ using degree of atypia
Recognize seborrheic keratosis features and distinguish from pigmented melanoma

questions.topic: Melanocytic nevi: junctional vs. compound vs. intradermal
questions.education_objective: Classify melanocytic nevi by location and understand nevus maturation

questions.topic: Dysplastic nevus vs. melanoma in situ
questions.education_objective: Distinguish dysplastic nevus from early melanoma using architectural and cytologic criteria
Recognize Spitz nevus features and distinguish from spitzoid melanoma

questions.topic: Melanoma subtypes: superficial spreading vs. nodular
questions.education_objective: Classify melanoma subtypes and correlate with prognosis and staging
Recognize desmoplastic melanoma and understand its diagnostic challenges

questions.topic: Viral infections: molluscum vs. verruca vulgaris
questions.education_objective: Distinguish molluscum contagiosum from verruca vulgaris using morphologic features
Differentiate herpes simplex from herpes zoster infections histologically
Identify dermatophyte infections using PAS staining and morphologic features
Distinguish epidermal cysts from trichilemmal cysts using wall characteristics
Differentiate hypertrophic scars from keloids histologically and clinically
Recognize solar elastosis and understand its significance in skin pathology
Recognize hidradenitis suppurativa histologic features and clinical correlation
Identify erythema nodosum as a septal panniculitis and understand its associations
Distinguish neutrophilic dermatoses and correlate with systemic associations
Identify systemic fungal infections in skin and distinguish morphologic features
Distinguish blue nevus from melanoma using architectural and cytologic criteria
Recognize porokeratosis morphology and understand its spectrum of variants
